label: SES-072 PI-045 code-changes implementation conversation: three slices (A, B, C) executed in sequence at Doug's terminal via Claude Code, producing the v2 MCP HTTP transport stack that the operational infrastructure from SES-071 was built around. Slice A added the `--transport {stdio,streamable-http}` CLI flag plus `--port` override, the `CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_HTTP_PORT` env-var setting (default 8810), and a parameterized `mcp_server.server.main()` dispatch; stdio remains the default and is byte-for-byte unchanged from prior behavior, preserving desktop-piped usage. Slice B added the shared-secret header middleware on the HTTP transport — `CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_SHARED_SECRET` env var, hard-fails at startup if streamable-http is selected without it; `SharedSecretMiddleware` in `mcp_server/middleware.py` validates `X-CRMBuilder-Secret` via hmac.compare_digest and returns HTTP 401 on missing or wrong values before route dispatch; stdio bypasses the middleware entirely. Slice C implemented DEC-205's engagement-marker fail-loud guard for the REST API: `api/marker_guard.py` with module-level `_MARKER_AT_START` and `EngagementMarkerGuardMiddleware`; `cli.run_api()` calls `set_marker_at_start(active_code)` after the existing engagement resolution; the middleware compares the live `current_engagement.json` reading to the captured value on every non-exempt request and returns HTTP 409 with the structured body `{error:'engagement_marker_changed', marker_at_start, marker_now, action:'restart API to bind to new engagement'}` plus a WARNING log line on mismatch; exempt paths `/health`, `/openapi.json`, `/docs`, `/redoc` bypass the guard. Slice C also added a `_UNINITIALIZED` sentinel (distinguishing 'marker not yet captured' from 'marker is None') and a `reset_marker_at_start_for_tests` helper. All three slices verified end-to-end against the live tunnel: slice A confirmed via FastMCP signature inspection and transport-dispatch tests; slice B confirmed via four-case curl matrix (no header → 401, wrong header → 401, correct header → 404 from FastMCP routing proving auth allowed the request through, missing-secret startup → RuntimeError exit=1); slice C confirmed via four-step end-to-end happy/drift/health-bypass/restart cycle on the live API (CRMBUILDER → SES-071 on happy path, marker flipped to CBM → HTTP 409 with the four expected body fields and WARNING log line, /health → 200 even mid-drift, restart re-binds to CBM and returns SES-001). Code-changes scope of PI-045 is structurally complete. Tests: 1489 total v2 tests collected at HEAD ae7a77ea; slice A added the dispatch tests, slice B added 8 tests across 2 files, slice C added 5 tests in test_marker_guard_middleware.py. No new architectural decisions surfaced; all three slices implemented behavior settled in SES-065 (the planning conversation's decide-and-announce items on port 8810, header X-CRMBuilder-Secret, etc.) and DEC-205 (engagement-marker fail-loud on drift, captured by the planning conversation). 1 session, 0 decisions, 0 planning items, 1 reference.

session.title: PI-045 code-changes implementation — slices A (transport flag + FastMCP HTTP binding), B (X-CRMBuilder-Secret middleware), C (engagement-marker fail-loud guard per DEC-205) executed in sequence at Doug's terminal via Claude Code; MCP HTTP transport now live on 127.0.0.1:8810 with two-layer authentication (Cloudflare Access edge + shared-secret app middleware) and DEC-205's drift guard on the REST API; PI-045 code-changes scope structurally complete
session.conversation_reference: Conceptual conversation comprising three sequential Claude Code sessions (one per slice) at Doug's terminal in the local crmbuilder clone, each driven by the corresponding `CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-pi-045-{A,B,C}-*.md` prompt authored in SES-065 (the PI-045 planning conversation). Operating mode: DETAIL per the kickoff's working-pattern section (code-writing slices, one-slice-at-a-time, full discussion and approval at each gate). The retrospective combined close-out is authored from the operational conversation's sandbox (SES-071's home) per Doug's option-1 election: a single session record covering slices A, B, C captures the work at session granularity with less bookkeeping overhead than three per-slice close-outs and matches how SES-071 captured PI-045 operational steps 1–4 as a single session. The three slice prompts themselves (in `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/`) remain the authoritative record of the per-slice scope, code changes, and verification criteria. Slice A landed at commit 95b801a2 ('v2: PI-045 slice A — tests for --transport flag and FastMCP HTTP binding'). Slice B landed at 209a3dc9 ('v2: PI-045 slice B — shared-secret header middleware on MCP HTTP transport'). Slice C landed at ea04e1a6 ('marker guard upgrade') with two follow-ups c578503b (_UNINITIALIZED sentinel + reset_marker_at_start_for_tests) and a9cfe138 (api/main.py adjustments). These code commits live in Doug's local clone and are pushed to origin on Doug's normal cadence (commit-and-push convention is Claude-Code-commits-Doug-pushes per the working-conventions rule in CLAUDE.md). The combined close-out's governance records (this payload + apply prompt) ship via the sandbox push convention separately. No new architectural decisions surfaced during the three slices — all implementation was directly downstream of SES-065's decide-and-announce items (port 8810 via CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_HTTP_PORT, header X-CRMBuilder-Secret via CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_SHARED_SECRET, secret storage at ~/.config/crmbuilder/mcp-secret mode 0600 sourced into a systemd-unit env var per SES-071's launchd→systemd substitution) and DEC-205 (engagement-marker fail-loud on drift via Starlette middleware comparing live marker to _MARKER_AT_START captured at process start; HTTP 409 with structured body on mismatch; restart required to bind to new engagement). Some implementation refinements emerged inside the per-slice scope without warranting new decisions: the _UNINITIALIZED sentinel in slice C cleanly disambiguates 'not yet captured' from 'captured-as-None'; a test helper reset_marker_at_start_for_tests enables clean test isolation; api/main.py middleware-registration adjustments tuned how the marker guard registers in the FastAPI app's middleware stack. Conversation closes with two files committed and pushed together in one sandbox commit: this close-out payload at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_072.json and the apply prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-072.md.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompts: each slice executed against its dedicated `CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-pi-045-{A,B,C}-*.md` prompt authored in SES-065. Slice A: 'PI-045 slice A — `--transport` flag and FastMCP HTTP binding' (operating mode DETAIL, predecessor none, successor slice B). Slice B: '...slice B — shared-secret middleware'. Slice C: '...slice C — engagement-marker fail-loud guard per DEC-205'.

Structured summary of the code-changes conversation across all three slices:

(1) Slice A — transport flag and FastMCP HTTP binding. Added `mcp_http_port: int = 8810` to `crmbuilder_v2/config.py`'s Settings class, env-var-driven via `CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_HTTP_PORT`, matching the existing `api_host` / `api_port` env-var pattern. Host hardcoded to `127.0.0.1` per DEC-202 — not exposed as a setting since making it user-configurable would invite a future foot-gun where it could be set to `0.0.0.0` and bypass DEC-202's MCP-only public exposure intent. Added `--transport {stdio,streamable-http}` and `--port <int>` argparse flags to the `crmbuilder-v2-mcp` CLI entry point in `crmbuilder_v2/cli.py`; defaults preserve existing desktop-piped behavior. Parameterized `mcp_server/server.py` `main()` to dispatch on transport — the call site that previously read `server.run('stdio')` at line 44 now reads `server.run(transport=transport, host=host, port=port)`. Tests in `tests/test_transport_dispatch.py` cover argparse choices (stdio default, streamable-http override, --port int conversion) and the build-and-dispatch path (verifies FastMCP.run is called with the right kwargs for each transport). No authentication in this slice — the HTTP transport accepts any unauthenticated request, useful only for local end-to-end smoke testing against the laptop until slice B lands.

(2) Slice B — shared-secret header middleware. Added `mcp_shared_secret: str | None = None` to Settings, env-var-driven via `CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_SHARED_SECRET`. Startup hard-fails (RuntimeError with the explicit message 'CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_SHARED_SECRET must be set when transport is streamable-http (DEC-204 second-layer auth)' and exit code 1) if streamable-http is selected without the secret being set — preserving DEC-204's two-layer authentication intent and preventing the server from accidentally going live without app-layer auth. New module `crmbuilder_v2/mcp_server/middleware.py` exposes `SharedSecretMiddleware` (Starlette-compatible) that validates the `X-CRMBuilder-Secret` request header against `Settings.mcp_shared_secret` via `hmac.compare_digest` (constant-time comparison; resistant to timing-oracle attacks). On missing or wrong header, the middleware returns HTTP 401 with JSON body `{"error":"unauthorized"}` before route dispatch; on match, the request passes through to FastMCP's routing. The middleware is registered on the streamable-http transport's underlying ASGI app; stdio transport bypasses the middleware entirely (no header to validate when the transport is a process pipe). Verification matrix run end-to-end: (i) `unset CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_SHARED_SECRET; crmbuilder-v2-mcp --transport streamable-http` → RuntimeError with the named message, exit code 1; (ii) `curl http://127.0.0.1:8813/` (no header) → HTTP 401 with `{"error":"unauthorized"}` and server log line 'rejected request GET / — missing or wrong X-CRMBuilder-Secret header'; (iii) `curl -H 'X-CRMBuilder-Secret: wrong' http://127.0.0.1:8813/` → same 401 + log; (iv) `curl -H 'X-CRMBuilder-Secret: $CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_SHARED_SECRET' -H 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream' http://127.0.0.1:8813/` → HTTP 404 ('Not Found') from FastMCP's routing — proves the middleware allowed the authenticated request through and the 401 vs 404 distinction is correctly middleware-vs-FastMCP-routing rather than a false positive. Slice B added 8 tests across 2 files (4 in `test_shared_secret_middleware.py`, 4 in `test_transport_dispatch.py` extending slice A's dispatch tests with auth-aware behavior). All 8 collected and passed in isolation.

(3) Slice C — engagement-marker fail-loud guard. Implements DEC-205's per-request fail-loud option (chosen in SES-065 over silent-follow after the planning conversation's codebase finding that `route_settings_to_engagement` is not a stat-and-read but a process-global state mutation incompatible with per-request rerouting). New module `crmbuilder_v2/api/marker_guard.py` with module-level `_MARKER_AT_START` capturing the engagement code that the API process bound to at startup, plus `EngagementMarkerGuardMiddleware` (Starlette-compatible) that on every non-exempt request stats the marker file `data/current_engagement.json`, reads the `engagement_code` field, compares to `_MARKER_AT_START`, and returns HTTP 409 with the structured body `{"error":"engagement_marker_changed","marker_at_start":"<captured>","marker_now":"<live>","action":"restart API to bind to new engagement"}` plus a WARNING log line `marker_guard: request <METHOD> <PATH> rejected — marker_at_start='<captured>' marker_now='<live>'` on mismatch. `cli.run_api()` calls `set_marker_at_start(active_code)` after the existing engagement resolution and before `uvicorn.run`, capturing the resolved active_code (which may have come from the `--engagement` CLI flag override or the marker file) — not the live marker; processes started with `--engagement` override stay bound to that engagement regardless of later marker changes. Exempt paths `/health`, `/openapi.json`, `/docs`, `/redoc` bypass the guard so liveness probes, OpenAPI consumers, and Swagger/ReDoc UIs continue to work mid-drift. Slice C's follow-up commit c578503b added a module-level `_UNINITIALIZED` sentinel object distinguishing 'marker not yet captured' from 'captured-as-None' (the latter being a legitimate state if the active_code happened to be None at startup — which shouldn't happen in practice but the type system can't rule out); the middleware's startup check now compares against the sentinel rather than `None`, and a `reset_marker_at_start_for_tests()` helper enables clean state reset across test cases. Slice C's second follow-up commit a9cfe138 tuned `api/main.py` middleware registration (specifically the middleware ordering within FastAPI's app initialization). Verification was end-to-end against the live API on port 8765: (i) happy path — marker says CRMBUILDER, `curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/sessions?limit=1` returns HTTP 200 with the CRMBUILDER engagement's most recent session SES-071 (PI-045 operational steps 1–4); (ii) drift path — while API still running, flipped marker to CBM via direct edit of `current_engagement.json`, then `curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/sessions?limit=1` returned HTTP 409 with the four expected body fields (error name, marker_at_start='CRMBUILDER', marker_now='CBM', action='restart API to bind to new engagement') and the WARNING log line naming both markers; (iii) health bypass — same drift state, `curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/health` returns HTTP 200 with `{"data":{"ok":true},"meta":{},"errors":null}` confirming the exempt-path bypass; (iv) restart fix — `kill $API_PID; wait; crmbuilder-v2-api &` (marker still CBM), then `curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/sessions?limit=1` returns HTTP 200 with CBM's SES-001 ('Methodology schemas — CBM content paper-test'), entirely different content from CRMBUILDER's SES-071, proving the restart re-bound the API process to CBM. Marker restored to CRMBUILDER (ENG-001) at the end. Slice C added 5 tests in `test_marker_guard_middleware.py` (4 from ea04e1a6 covering match/mismatch/missing-marker/exempt-bypass plus 1 `test_uninitialized_guard_bypasses` from c578503b's sentinel work). All 5 collected and passed in isolation. Full v2 collect-only count at HEAD ae7a77ea: 1489 tests.

(4) Three two-headed observations spanning the slices. First: the working tree picked up unrelated commits during the conversation (audit-v1.2 Prompts F and G; PI-030 slice A) — these landed on main since slice C started but did not collide with PI-045's identifier space or code surface; they're noted only because they appear in the linear history between this conversation's slice-B HEAD (209a3dc9) and slice-C HEAD (ea04e1a6). Second: the slice C prompt's Done block asked for a 'pointer that PI-045's code-changes scope is now complete' and named the path under `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/` where the operational step-5 conversation's kickoff prompt 'should land' — but slice C did not itself author that kickoff. The step-5 kickoff is therefore still to-be-authored (captured in in_flight_at_end). Third: this combined close-out is authored from the operational-conversation sandbox (SES-071's home) rather than from a fresh code-changes sandbox; option 1 per the SES-072 authoring agreement during the operational-conversation thread. This bundling treats the three Claude Code coding sessions as a single conceptual code-changes conversation with one governance record at the end — symmetric with how SES-071 captured PI-045 operational steps 1–4 as a single record and matches the kickoff's framing of 'the code-changes implementation conversation' as a single conversation.

(5) Identifier rebase. At authoring time the v2 governance heads were SES-071 / DEC-225 / PI-048 / COP-071 / DEP-050 / references=851. This close-out claims SES-072. No new decisions are surfaced (everything implemented in this conversation was downstream of pre-settled decisions); no new planning items are surfaced. The single payload reference is `session:SES-072 -[is_about]-> planning_item:PI-045`. Standard sandbox commit-and-push: payload + apply prompt committed and pushed together as one commit.
session.artifacts_produced: Code commits in Doug's local clone (pushed to origin on Doug's normal cadence): slice A at 95b801a2 ('v2: PI-045 slice A — tests for --transport flag and FastMCP HTTP binding'), slice B at 209a3dc9 ('v2: PI-045 slice B — shared-secret header middleware on MCP HTTP transport'), slice C at ea04e1a6 ('marker guard upgrade') with follow-ups c578503b ('_UNINITIALIZED sentinel + reset_marker_at_start_for_tests') and a9cfe138 ('api/main.py adjustments'). New files: `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/mcp_server/middleware.py` (SharedSecretMiddleware), `crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/api/marker_guard.py` (EngagementMarkerGuardMiddleware + _MARKER_AT_START + _UNINITIALIZED + set_marker_at_start + reset_marker_at_start_for_tests). Modified files: `config.py` (mcp_http_port, mcp_shared_secret), `mcp_server/server.py` (parameterized main()), `cli.py` (--transport / --port args; set_marker_at_start call from run_api), `api/main.py` (middleware registration). New test files: `tests/test_transport_dispatch.py`, `tests/test_shared_secret_middleware.py`, `tests/test_marker_guard_middleware.py`. Total new tests across all three slices: at least 14 (slice A's transport-dispatch coverage plus slice B's 8 plus slice C's 5 — counts approximate per slice C's report). Total v2 test count at HEAD=ae7a77ea: 1489 (collect-only). In the crmbuilder repo: this close-out payload at `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_072.json` and the apply prompt at `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-072.md`.
session.in_flight_at_end: Three follow-on items queued for PI-045 to reach Complete status: (1) The operational step-5 conversation's kickoff prompt is still to-be-authored under `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/`. Slice C's prompt named this as 'should land' but slice C did not author it. The kickoff covers Doug registering `https://mcp.crmbuilder.ai` in claude.ai's MCP integration with the three custom headers (`CF-Access-Client-Id`, `CF-Access-Client-Secret`, `X-CRMBuilder-Secret`); the operational infrastructure from SES-071 plus the code-changes from this conversation make step 5 unblocked. Suggested filename: `pi-045-step-5-claude-ai-mcp-integration-registration-kickoff.md` or similar. (2) Pre-step-5 hygiene: rotate the `claude-ai-mcp` Service Token's Client Secret per the followup named in SES-071's in_flight_at_end — the Client Secret value appeared in this conversation's predecessor (SES-071, the operational conversation) during a doubled-header diagnostic; blast radius is now becoming non-zero because slice B's middleware is in place and the MCP HTTP transport is buildable. Rotation is a Cloudflare Zero Trust → Access controls → Service credentials → Service Tokens → claude-ai-mcp → Refresh; capture new Client ID + Client Secret atomically with `printf '%s' '<value>' > ~/.config/crmbuilder/cf-access-client-{id,secret}`; update canonical copies in Doug's password manager. The shared-secret value at `~/.config/crmbuilder/mcp-secret` does not need rotation here — that value has not appeared in any conversation transcript. (3) After step 5 lands, the smoke-test conversation runs the full 44-MCP-tool surface end-to-end against both CRMBUILDER and CBM engagements (per the PI-045 kickoff's part (c) and (d)), ships the documentation deliverables at `crmbuilder-v2/docs/remote-deployment.md` and the `crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md` Tier-2 entry naming the remote MCP URL, and authors its own close-out that advances PI-045 status from In Progress to Complete.
